stall_name: Riverside Good Food (Zion Riverside Food Centre)
reviews: Lard after lard, and spoonful of sweet chilli, this plate of Hokkien Mee at $4 is definitely a steal! Wet and gooey, the seafood broth is really fragrant. The prawn is surprisingly fresh, given its size and price. Note that there's always a long queue during lunch and dinner hours. Go early or go late!
contact_info: 70 Zion Road, #01-20 Zion Riverside Food Centre, Singapore 247792

reviews.author: Asia Apparel
reviews.text: This is one of the best hawker centre in Singapore. Has the highest ratio of best rated food here. Its clean airy and toilet are extremely clean too. The most famous food here is the Zion Char Kuey Teow which we been having for more than 14 years. Crispy pork lard, fresh cockles large portion and super charr black sauced Kuey Teow. The most sinful meal in town. The Hainanese chicken rice is also superb quality. The duck rice kuey chap is delicious. Braised just right tender. Wantan mee also is very good. Prawn noodles as well. Even the carrot cake , beef noodle is also top quality. Spoilt for choice. However the parking is so hard limited and no shelter walk way. You will get stuck if it's rain. Parking is really hard. Come 10 times 9.5 times no parking. However this place is highly recommended. It's the top 5 hawker centre in my list. These days have to come at super off peak timing but many shop will be rest hours.

reviews.author: PJ Moorthy
reviews.text: Had a couple of Michelin star food. The Char Keay Teow was very good & I queued for 20 mins for it.   The 2 elderly buskers played & sang nice old songs that was appreciated by the crowd having dinner.

reviews.author: Eileen Kwek
reviews.text: Bought 红油抄手 from a beef noodle shop.  OMG, every dumpling is so big and the sauce is so savoury with black vinegar.  Best I have ever eaten!

reviews.author: Shaolin Leong
reviews.text:
A relatively small food center.
2km from where we stay & we always jogged here have dinner & walk back .
The famous CKT is "so so" to us , with terrible long waiting time.Not worth the wait ! The wanton noodles is not bad surprisingly! BKT is ok too ! Prawn noodle at the corner has loyal supporters too !
Yet to try the rest ! Parking I heard is a headache  !

reviews.author: Crimsonpig101
reviews.text: A busy foodcourt which is pretty hidden away, 25+ food stalls. No air-conditioning just wall fans. Tried the Michelin guide stir fry noodles for medium size $8 which were good but was it worth the queue time?

stall_name: No:18 Zion Road Fried Kway Teow (Zion Riverside Food Centre)
reviews: What u came to Zion Riverside 4. A popular store here at ZRFC. Be prepared to wait at least 30-45 mins. CKT here is wet version, very sweet. Worth the calories.
Char Kway Teow. A sinful messy plate of greasy black gooey noodles with a few measly cockles and some sliced lup cheong (Chinese sausage) but it's oh so good.
Char Kway Teow. There is wok hey and the portion is rather decent. This is the wet version which I normally like, but unfortunately, I found it to be too sweet.
CKT @ Zion Road. Though the smallest portion out of the menu, it didn't fail to satisfy. The noodles were fried with just the right amount of lard and 'wok-hei'.

Char Kway Teow (SGD $5) @ No. 18 Zion Road Fried Kway Teow. Hawker Ho Kian Tat serves a messy-looking but delicious version. The kway teow and yellow wheat noodles are soft and moist, with a smoky aroma.
Char Kway Teow ($6 for medium). Full of flavor wok hei goodness. This version is more on the sweet and wet side. Topped with generous amount of ingredients.
contact_info: 70 Zion Road, #01-17 Zion Riverside Food Centre, Singapore 247792

google.google_name: No. 18 Zion Road Fried Kway Teow

reviews.author: Asia Apparel
reviews.text: Still the place to go to for a quality plate of ckt after 2 decades of eating here. The $6 version already very big. I ordered my usual $8 size + $3 more cockles. Crispy pork lard. Still very nice just abit dryer than last time. Overall still one of the best ckt in Singapore. These days always come at off peak ard 3pm to avoid the long queue. However take note 4pm to 5pm boss always take a short break. Being a previous owner of 4 full fledge restaurants, food is either hit or miss on different day mood on different people with different taste expectations. However I want to say the cleanliness of the kitchen is exceptional.

reviews.author: Sean
reviews.text:
I came on a Monday, off peak hours after five pm.  There was a handful of people queueing.  I wasn't sure what was the operating time but they only switched on the lights at 530 pm and then customers started to get their orders.

The prices are fairly expensive at 6, 8 and 10 SGD. In my picture you could see the difference in size between the six dollars and the eight dollars.  They do accept qr code payment with various merchants.

The dish was moist to the right extent.  Slightly spicy.  I wondered why they never asked if I wanted it spicy.

I could taste the wok hei, the various ingredients including pork lard and Chinese sausage.  The cockles were fresh but rather small.  For me personally, I would have preferred the dish to be sweeter.

The lady helper seemed to be  foreigner.  Both she and the uncle chef were without smiles and that was why I rated service as average.

Overall it was a satisfying meal.

1 Dec 2025

reviews.author: Shaolin Leong
reviews.text:
When you waited 30 minutes for a plate of CKT you will definitely raise your standards for your CKT.........
More to the moist & wet type. Wokhey is not fantastic. Slightly salty. $6/$8/$10 per plate.
Which is expensive.
Precision & meticulous cooking steps by owner resulting in long waiting time. Final product is slightly above average.....
Honestly so so ....therefore not a die die must try thing like what the review said ! No crispy pork lard. Not oily. Garlic fragrance is there. Over all "还好而已"！
Hong Lim market CKT still better.That is why grandpa loved them ! Learn & listen to our seniors & elders! Don't reinvent the wheel !!!

Outrum CKT is still way better !!! Even the CKT stall at Redhill market"自己人" is better than theirs ! Dry & yet with wokhey !

Finally , not all Michelin are good stuff ! And not all good stuff are Mitchelin ! I say one !!! 😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤😤

reviews.author: TPH
reviews.text:
Located in Zion road hawker Ctr  this Stall(no 18, Zion road fried kway teow) is one of the best if not the best Singapore stye fried CKT- I love to eat here even tho long queue. Ate $8 plate with extra $1 egg . Very substantial amount,.
Nice wok Hei , slightly wet type n slightly sweetish - typical Spore stye fried with black sweet thick  soya sauce
Highly recommended

reviews.author: FTS
reviews.text: Excellent CKT, went there just before they opened for late lunch. Was already a long Q. I ordered a Large when a med size would have more than enough for me. For my tastes this is the best hawker CKT in Singapore. Read reviews of how they could be rude but I did not observe this.

stall_name: Boon Tong Kee Kway Chap Braised Duck (Zion Riverside Food Centre)
reviews: Gave this a go after spotting a surprisingly long queue. The kway was smooth and silky, soaked in a light herbal broth. The platter of braised items was generous.
Since March 2016, Madam Yang's rendition of this traditional Teochew dish has been a standout. The duck is tender and well-infused with the braising liquid.
Boon Tong Kee is 1 of my favourite kway chap store. The sauce is flavorful without being overly salty. The ingredients are always cleaned thoroughly.
Michelin bib and for good reason, they're special. The rice sheets (kway) are delicate and the herbal soup is not overwhelming.
Featured in the Michelin Guide, BTK’s Kway Chao Braised Duck features a rich, dark gravy. The pig trotters and duck are particularly well-marinated.
contact_info: 70 Zion Road, #01-24 Zion Riverside Food Centre, Singapore 247792. Phone: +65 91849881

reviews.author: eYeWee
reviews.text: Very popular hawker centre and a lot of stalls close for cny. 2019 Michelin so I gave it a try. The super slow moving queue is really unbelievable, the take order guy kept asking if want to add this that and only when complete conversation it’s share to the one inside who chop slowly. Also he must pack finish and collect money then he ask for next order. Never in my life I had seen such a slow teamwork. That aside… the rice is a lot but hard… the duck is ok. Still no way I’m gonna queue for this ever, no way.

reviews.author: Grace Law
reviews.text: Food was great! Easy parking across at Great World City. Just wished the queue moved faster. Seemed super popular too.

reviews.author: KC PSbackup
reviews.text:
Generous serving $18 mixed meats for 2 pax with $2 add-on.

Try it with different chilli sauces.  Duck was firm tender 8/10. Pig skin was perfect no bad porky smell, firm and had my collagen fix 8/10.  Pork belly had more meat than fat firm well braised 8/10.  Tau kuah was softer and smoother than most.  Offals were great no offensive porky odour 9/10.   Great food.  Nice young guy tending stall polite friendly. Generous will sambal.

Will be back.

reviews.author: S Wun
reviews.text: Nov 2025: will definitely go again. Generous thick cuts of duck meat. The meat was moist and not tough. Gravy was also “al dente” in thickness yet not too thick.

reviews.author: Stanley Pang
reviews.text: Thick braised is nice and savory. I couldn't be sure but I thought I tasted marmite.

stall_name: Zion Road Big Prawn Noodle (Zion Riverside Food Centre)
reviews: Prawn Noodle. Fresh and huge prawns in a flavourful broth packed with umami goodness. The soup is peppery and rich, definitely one of the best in Singapore.
13nett portion. U can only choose the price. We were lucky cos there's no normal 13nett prawns left so they gave us the huge ones. The broth is spectacular.
8nett. Gets you the most normal prawns, but still of reasonable size. The soup is consistent - thick and dark.
Prawn Noodles Dry ($13 portion). Seeing those huge Prawns displayed at the front makes it impossible to resist. The dry version comes with a bowl of that signature soup.
Prawn Mee | $8. I’ve had my fair share of prawn noodles from this particular stall. The quality is always top-notch, especially the broth which is boiled for hours.
Huge prawns, rich peppery broth, what more could you ask for? The queue is long but moves fast.
contact_info: 70 Zion Road, #01-04 Zion Riverside Food Centre, Singapore 247792

google.google_name: Zhi Wei Xian Zion Road Big Prawn Noodle 知味鲜

reviews.author: Rick
reviews.text: Good balanced prawn and pork broth. Nice chilli mix with ketchup .... some may not like the ketchup but I guess you can ask him to skip that. I ordered the $20 version, comes with 3 large prawns sliced into halves, 3 pieces pork ribs and some pork belly. My favourite place for large prawns noodles ... Better than Adam Road one.

reviews.author: 葉
reviews.text:
It is flavour city with this one. The chewy,  thick bee hoon and yellow noodles are tossed in a savoury sauce with hints of ketchup and sambal. Prawns are fresh, succulent, and sweet with a satisfying bite, unlike the smaller ones commonly used. Dip them into a little soy sauce and chili powder for extra shiokness. At times, the combination of prawns with dry noodles even tasted like Singapore’s famous chili crab. Crab can also be ordered a la carte here.

The soup is the star here: full of depth and oomph, rich with the sweet essence of seafood and pork, and of course, umami. The pork ribs also deserve praise for its peppery aroma and tender texture. Bursts of fat come through when a piece of fried pork lard is bitten into - sinfully divine.

reviews.author: Rennie Lee
reviews.text: One of the better prawn noodles around! We got the dry version which also comes with some soup. Soup is rich and tasty! Generous with the toppings, 2 pork ribs and a good amount of prawns (sliced in half). Dry version is good too!! Slightly pricey but okay as the amount of ingredients is a lot! Mine is the $8 one

reviews.author: C K Leong (Ah Leong San)
reviews.text: It’s been a while since I ate here. This is one of few stalls selling BIG prawns noodles only. Minimum price starts at $8 as seen in photo. Besides the big prawns there are pork ribs and pork belly slices in it. Soup is very intense and flavourful. Prawns are definitely fresh and delicious. Pork ribs are tender enough to fall off the bone when bitten upon. Pork belly slices are thinly cut and actually firm to bite. Love the whole meal. Only setback is stall owner is the silent type. So can come across as aloft. I know service for hawker food is not highly sought but a simple reply is at least he could’ve done. Nevertheless I’ll go back when I feel like eating big prawns noodles. By the way cash is king here. So don’t try paying with other ways.

reviews.author: Paul Lui
reviews.text:
Ordered 2 big tiger prawns with pork Rib & meat @ dry version.
Dry noodles 🍜 comes with slight chilly paste and taste was acceptable. The soup was tasty with soothing prawns umani flavour.
